title: No symbol for interface alias rhs (interface instantiated in generate block)
body:
The following SV file produces an internal Verilator error:

```
interface iface (input logic clk);
    logic valid;
    modport sink (input clk, input valid);
endinterface

module leaf (iface.sink S);
    logic q;
    always_ff @(posedge S.clk) q <= S.valid;
endmodule

module middle (iface.sink S);
    leaf u (.S(S));
endmodule

module top #(parameter bit P = 0) (input logic clk);
    generate
        if (P) begin : g
            iface s (.clk(clk));
        end else begin : g
            iface s (.clk(clk));
        end
    endgenerate

    middle u (.S(g.s));
endmodule
```

Based on my investigation, it seems like the following conditions are required to trigger the error:

1. Interface instantiated inside a generate block.
2. That interface connected to a module via modport port.
3. That module passes the interface down to a sub-module.

The example is only really sensible when the generate blocks are given the same name, but this isn't actually required to reproduce the error.

The error I get is:
```
$ verilator --cc verilator_bug_repro.sv 
%Error: Internal Error: verilator_bug_repro.sv:24:25: ../V3LinkDot.cpp:2686: No symbol for interface alias rhs
   24 | module leaf (iface.sink S);
      |                         ^
                        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.047 for more assistance.
```

The full output with --debug is:
